experiment: Functional symmetry and multi-channel bilateral invariance
description: Tests sensitivity of Chavez Transform to time-reversal symmetry (s -> 1-s) and verifies Block Replication invariance across patterns for 16D zero sequences.

functional_equation_signal.description: Chavez Transform magnitude comparison for different zero encodings. Lower value = higher structural symmetry alignment.

multi_channel_invariance.description: Confirmation of Block Replication invariance for 16D zero sequence across Canonical Six patterns.

discontinuous_gateway_probe.description: ZDTP transmission through Gateway #3 (S3B) - the discontinuous sedenion map - for the 16D zero sequence.

interpretation: The transform magnitude drops by two orders of magnitude for anti-symmetric pairs, demonstrating that the Chavez Transform directly detects the functional symmetry of Riemann zeros. Multi-channel invariance is perfect (variance=0.0), confirming the zeros form a rigid structural sequence. Robustness through Gateway S3B confirms the sequence's topological stability.
phase_70_relevance: Validates the use of Chavez Transform as a detector for the Riemann Functional Equation symmetry (Route 2). Confirming invariance supports the scalar reduction in Route 3.
